title: Web Portal Evaluation
description: Comprehensive evaluation of the SnapBack web portal implementation
executive_summary: The SnapBack web portal is a Next.js 14+ application with a modern architecture using App Router, Server Components, and oRPC for API communication. It features a marketing site and SaaS dashboard with comprehensive metrics display, AI detection statistics, and activity feeds. The implementation follows best practices for performance, security, and user experience.

architecture.routing: File-system based routing with marketing and SaaS layouts
architecture.data_fetching: React Query with TanStack Query for client-side caching

features: Metrics grid with snapshot counts, recoveries, files protected, and AI detection rate

MetricsGrid.description: Bento grid layout showing key metrics with animated number tickers
features: Files protected with file check icon
AI detection rate with sparkles icon

AIDetectionStats.description: Card showing AI tool detection statistics

features: Different icons for each activity type

tables.feature_usage: Tracking of feature usage by AI tools

optimizations: React Query caching with configurable stale times
Code splitting with Next.js automatic optimization
Image optimization with Next.js Image component

caching.client_side: React Query with 1-2 minute stale times
caching.server_side: Database query optimization with denormalized fields

security.data_protection: Privacy-first approach with metadata-only storage by default
security.api_security: Type-safe oRPC endpoints with input validation

ux_features.animations: Framer Motion and Aceternity UI components

testing.unit_tests: Unit tests for components and hooks

positive: Modern Next.js architecture with App Router
Good error handling and loading states
concerns: Some components have complex nested structures
Some mock data usage in AI detection stats

recommendations: Implement comprehensive unit and integration tests for all dashboard components
Add more detailed documentation for the data layer and API endpoints
Consider implementing real-time updates with WebSockets for activity feed
Add more granular permissions for team/organization features
